title: Runtime Boundaries
description: createRuntime mounts a component tree as its own sovereign runtime: its own ledger, its own lock, its own failure domain. Stacks spawn stacks — the deployer and the deployed are the same substance.
sovereignty_model_body: A runtime boundary is a line in the component tree with precise semantics. Everything inside it belongs to a child universe; everything outside sees only a single node.
aspect_ledger_desc: The child persists to its own stack, named by the wrapper node's path+key address. Omit memory to inherit the parent's backend; supply one for a fully sovereign ledger.
aspect_lock_desc: The child runtime acquires its own deployment lock on its own stack name — concurrent deploys of the same child universe are excluded independently of the parent.
aspect_failure_desc: Child deployment failure and lock-acquisition failure surface on the wrapper node's outputs (status, error, ready) as data. A child universe can never throw through the parent's executor.
aspect_sealed_context_desc: Parent providers are invisible inside the boundary. The root component's props are the entire boundary contract, statically typed.
aspect_crosses_props_desc: Props pass through verbatim — values, callbacks, accessors — exactly as at any component boundary. Receiver contracts govern; there is no introspection, per-prop tracking, or snapshotting.
reactivity_body: In-process reactivity crosses the boundary through the shared tracking graph: pass an accessor as a prop and the child's resources re-run when it changes, while each runtime's flush reconciles only its own tree. Values a child resource consumes persist in the child's ledger through the same point-of-use serialization as everywhere else — there is no boundary-level persistence mechanism.
recursion_body: The deployer and the deployed are the same substance. A CReact app is a runtime managing resources; createRuntime makes a runtime one of those resources. The wrapper is an ordinary component in the Show/For family, built entirely on useAsyncOutput and render() — no renderer intrinsics. Nesting composes to arbitrary depth, and one identity scheme addresses every level: the wrapper node's path+key address is the child's stack name.
detach_vs_destroy_body: Removing the JSX (or disposing the parent) detaches the child: its cleanup functions run, its runtime stops, and its ledger persists untouched. Re-mounting re-hydrates from that ledger and re-converges. Destroying a child universe is only ever an explicit act against its own Memory — never a side effect of removing JSX.
warning_teardown: Deleting a wrapper node tears down the wrapper's handler — not the child's resources. If a child universe should release real infrastructure, drive that from inside the child (or against its Memory) before detaching.
publishing_node_body: A node with a stable address is a public interface. Because addresses are deterministic (resource path + key), another app — or a later boot of the same app — can read a node's outputs straight from the ledger without sharing any code.
cross_ledger_body: Two apps that don't share code can still share state: one reads the other's ledger through the Memory interface and serves the values it finds as reactive outputs.
remote_transport_body: The Memory interface is the wire format. Implement it over any transport — HTTP, a queue, a database — and a runtime on one machine can host the ledger of a universe running on another. The runtime never knows the difference.
